channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok goes dark as U.S. ban takes effect
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Millions of TikTok users in the United States are no longer able to watch videos on the social media platform as a federal ban on the immensely popular app takes effect.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Independent professionals are all about value for money when it comes to choosing web hosting, but still value sustainability
item.description: Independent professionals prioritize cost-effective, high-performance web hosting, but sustainability is increasingly important.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Health care AI, intended to save money, turns out to require a lot of expensive humans
item.description: Artificial intelligence systems require consistent monitoring and staffing to put in place and to keep them working well.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: That Apple ID disabled message? It's a dangerous scam
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy breaks down why those emails that warn that "Your Apple ID has been disabled" is most likely a scam. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: A rare wave of U.S.-China camaraderie broke out online in recent days as “refugees” from the popular short video platform TikTok poured onto a Chinese social media platform to protest a now-delayed ban on the service.
item.dc:creator: Fu Ting And David Cohen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bitcoin soars past $100,000 ahead of possible early action on crypto by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The price of bitcoin topped $100,000 again early Friday as a pumped up cryptocurrency industry expects early action by Donald Trump when he's sworn in as president next week.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden orders review, new rules governing US national cybersecurity
item.description: President Biden has introduced a list of stringent security measures for the federal government and software providers to adhere to, or lose their contracts.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Windows 10 to Windows 11 Transition: What You Need to Know
item.description: Microsoft's support for Windows 10 ends on October 14, 2025, but the switch to Windows 11 is happening slowly due to stricter hardware requirements. Here's what you need to know about the transition and its impact on Microsoft-365 apps and Office versions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What everyone’s missing about Android upgrades in 2025
item.description: When we talk about Android upgrades, it’s all too easy to miss the forest for the trees. It’s a familiar tale here in the land o’ Googley matters — and with some wacky-seeming changes on the way for Android upgrades in 2025, the myopic musings are only getting louder.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia presenta nuevos microservicios de protección para la IA agéntica
item.description: Nvidia ha incorporado a sus herramientas de software Nvidia NeMo Guardrails nuevos microservicios de inferencia (NIM) para robustecer la seguridad de la inteligencia artificial (IA).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 3 big predictions for AI in financial services in 2025
item.description: As we look ahead to 2025, the financial services landscape will be shaped not by AI as a standalone innovation, but by how it enables smarter, faster, and more personalized experiences. Here are three key predictions for how AI will transform the industry: 1.
item.dc:creator: Ben Borodach, Fast Company

item.title: Americans keep falsely claiming this random man from Vancouver is RedNote’s CEO
item.description: Americans flocking to the Chinese app RedNote thought they’d been welcomed by the platform’s CEO. Turns out, he’s just a guy from Vancouver. “It was a typical Sunday,” Jerry tells Fast Company. “I was home alone, browsing RedNote as usual.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Biden's big semiconductor law will ramp up US chip production -- but at a high cost, report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sweeping 2022 law, touted by President Joe Biden as a way to revive U.S. manufacturing of semiconductors and reduce the country’s reliance on foreign-made computer chips, will “sharply increase production’’ of semiconductors in the United States.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg will cohost reception with Republican billionaires for Trump inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is cohosting a reception with billionaire Republican donors next week for Donald Trump’s inauguration, the latest sign of the Facebook founder's embrace of the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: AI experts tamp down hype, expect challenges for 2025
item.description: A trio of AI researchers and experts in Lithuania and England have tossed some cold water on the hype surrounding generative AI and predict that in 2025, companies and investors will have to lower their expectations regarding what the technology and large language models (LLM) can do.
item.dc:creator: Network World
